content:
Navigating Small Business Grants in Ontario

Ontario provides a highly diverse regional funding environment. Managed by the Ontario Ministry of Economic Development, Job Creation and Trade, and federal organizations like FedDev Ontario, active businesses can qualify for capital loans, training credits, and voucher programs.

Ontario Regional Funding Directory Map

Funding availability inside Ontario depends heavily on your geographic location. Use this table to target the agency serving your region:

Geographic Region Regional Funding Body Primary Focus Area
Southern / GTA Ontario FedDev Ontario Advanced manufacturing scale-up, automation, and technology adoption loans
Northern Ontario FedNor Community tourism, mineral/raw processing, and remote communications development
Rural / Eastern / Southwestern Regional Development Program (RDP) Capital expenditure matching and regional infrastructure grants

"Who should NOT apply?" (Ontario Exclusions)

To avoid administrative loss, check these key exclusion categories before drafting proposals:

  • Unincorporated Businesses: Major regional capital matching programs (such as RDP) are restricted to registered, incorporated enterprises. Unincorporated sole proprietorships are generally ineligible.
  • Retroactive Training Claims: For the Canada-Ontario Job Grant (COJG), you cannot claim training program fees or course enrollments that began before receiving a signed approval letter.
  • Refinancing Debt: Provincial grant and loan frameworks cannot be used to pay off existing credit lines or commercial real estate leases.

True Freshness: Active 2026 Ontario Intakes

Align your commercial planning with these active channels for the 2026 fiscal year:

  • Canada-Ontario Job Grant (COJG): Active intakes covering up to 83% of training costs, up to $15,000 per trainee for qualified upskilling courses.
  • Starter Company Plus: Provides up to $5,000 in non-repayable grants and mentoring for young businesses via local Small Business Enterprise Centres (SBECs).
  • Ontario Centres of Innovation (OCI) Voucher Program: Active vouchers matching collaborative industry-academic research projects.

Ontario Budget Corporate Tax Cut Context

In addition to active grant programs, the 2026 Ontario Budget has proposed reducing the Small Business Corporate Income Tax rate from 3.2% to 2.2% (effective July 1, 2026). If enacted, this tax rate cut will improve net operational cash flow for small businesses operating as Canadian-Controlled Private Corporations (CCPCs) with active business income below $500,000.

Estimated Program Timelines

Plan your funding timeline based on these standard evaluation periods in Ontario:

Program Channel Approval Speed Payout Schedule
Canada-Ontario Job Grant (COJG) 4 - 6 Weeks Reimbursement after training logs are submitted
Regional Development Program (RDP) 3 - 6 Months Progressive milestone releases against verified invoices
Starter Company Plus 8 - 12 Weeks Two-part grant release following business plan review

Topical Stacking: Stacking COJG with Federal R&D Support

Coordinating provincial and federal resources is highly effective. If you are developing custom software, apply for the provincial COJG grant to retrain your team on advanced databases. Once the training program concludes and active development begins, claim the developer payroll lines under the federal SR&ED program. By combining these channels, you can offset up to 75-85% of eligible technical labor costs under ideal conditions.

Next Steps

Identify your regional funding opportunities. Run our free eligibility quiz or compare the IRAP R&D wage guides to build your complete provincial and federal funding schedule.
shortAnswer: Yes — Ontario small businesses can access regional capital support (RDP matching grants), workforce training credits (COJG matching up to 83% / $15,000 per trainee), and municipal Starter Company Plus grants ($5,000). These programs stack with federal resources like IRAP and SR&ED to cover a substantial portion of R&D costs.
shortAnswerQuestion: What small business grants and funding are available in Ontario for 2026?

faq.question: What is the Canada-Ontario Job Grant (COJG) limit?
faq.answer: COJG covers up to 50% to 83% of eligible workforce training fees (such as course tuition and books), capped at a maximum of $15,000 per trainee.

faq.question: Are Starter Company Plus grants repayable?
faq.answer: No. Starter Company Plus grants of up to $5,000 are non-repayable, but require completion of a structured business training curriculum and business plan review.

faq.question: What is the proposed corporate tax rate change for Ontario small businesses?
faq.answer: The 2026 Ontario Budget proposed reducing the small business corporate tax rate from 3.2% to 2.2% (effective July 1, 2026) for qualifying CCPCs on active business income up to $500,000.

expertTip.title: Apply Through Your Local SBEC
expertTip.content: Starter Company Plus and local entrepreneurship support are managed directly by regional Small Business Enterprise Centres (SBECs). Book an introductory call with an advisor to review requirements before submitting.
